summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is electric current?

Key Concepts — Current, Resistance, & Ohm’s Law Electric Current Current is the rate of flow of charge: I = Q/t. Conventional current flows from + to − (opposite to electron flow). The other options — 'Charge times time', 'Voltage divided by power', and 'Resistance times voltage' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the SI unit of resistance?

Key Concepts — Current, Resistance, & Ohm’s Law Electric Current Current is the rate of flow of charge: I = Q/t. Ohm's Law V = IR The voltage across a resistor equals current times resistance. The other options — 'Volt (V)', 'Ampere (A)', and 'Watt (W)' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the formula for electrical power?

Power — P = IV = I²R = V²/R. The other options — 'P = I/V', 'P = V/I', and 'P = IR' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is a non-ohmic device?

Non-ohmic: diodes, filament bulbs (resistance changes with current/temperature). The other options — 'One that follows Ohm's Law', 'Any battery', and 'A perfect conductor' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
